question_title: Will the interest in “uss abraham lincoln” change between 2026-03-03 and 2026-03-12 according to Google Trends?
summary: The search term "uss abraham lincoln" spiked to 100 on Mar 2, 2026 due to Operation Epic Fury (US-Iran war) and Iran's claims of striking the carrier with ballistic missiles. By Mar 3, it had already dropped to 42 — a 58% single-day decline. The question asks whether the value on Mar 12 will be within ±3 of the Mar 3 value. Post-spike decay is the dominant dynamic: specific military asset searches decay rapidly as attention shifts to broader conflict terms ("Iran war", "Operation Epic Fury"). The narrow "Doesn't change" band [39, 45] is very hard to hit 9 days later. The ongoing conflict provides small probability of a new carrier-specific incident that could re-spike interest, but this must be timed specifically near Mar 12 to matter. Comparable Iran-conflict Google Trends questions show 75-95% P(Decreases) with similar dynamics.

factors.description: Post-spike decay pattern: Value dropped from 100 to 42 in one day (58% drop). For news-driven spikes, the trajectory continues declining to 5-15% of peak over 5-10 days, putting Mar 12 well below 39.

Specific entity search decay: During prolonged conflicts, people shift from specific asset names ('USS Abraham Lincoln') to broader terms ('Iran war'). The carrier-specific search decays faster than general conflict interest.
Narrow 'Doesn't change' band: [39, 45] is only 7 points wide. Hitting this exact range 9 days after a peak is highly improbable given typical post-spike volatility.
Iran's carrier-strike claim drove the peak: The sensational claim (denied by CENTCOM) was a one-time viral moment that won't recur in the same way.

Comparable forecast calibration: Similar Iran-conflict Google Trends questions (e.g., 'khamenei' at 85%, 'reza pahlavi' at 87%) show 75-95% P(Decreases) for similar decay rates and threshold distances.
Ongoing active conflict provides small chance of new carrier-specific incident that could re-spike interest near Mar 12. War expected to last 4-5 weeks and carrier remains deployed.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases (86%)**

Key reasoning:
- The "uss abraham lincoln" search term spiked to 100 on Mar 2 due to Operation Epic Fury and Iran's (denied) claim of hitting the carrier with missiles
- It already dropped 58% to 42 on Mar 3 — a steep single-day decline
- Post-spike decay for specific military asset searches is rapid as attention shifts to broader war terms
- By Mar 12, the value will very likely be well below 39 (the "Decreases" threshold)
- The narrow "Doesn't change" band [39-45] is only 7 points wide, hard to hit 9 days out
- The ongoing war provides a small (~7%) chance of a new carrier-specific spike, but it would need to be timed near Mar 12
- Comparable Iran-conflict trend questions show 85-95% P(Decreases) with similar dynamics

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| Decreases | 86% |
| Increases | 7% |
| Doesn't change | 7% |
